reviews.review_msg: I would avoid taking classes with him but he's tolerable. He's a bright guy and knows the subject but cannot organize the course in a teachable way. His notes are just awful. He gives 'skeleton notes' which amount to 10 words on a page and expects you to listen and fill everything in during lectures. Please create complete and useful notes!
my advice to you, don't take course with evans unless you wanna kill you gpa. asta la vista gpa .... booom!
The worest prof I have ever met. Nobody understands his humour. His voice is very low and you could just hear the first word of his sentence. Don't choose his course. FOR YOUR SAFETY, PLEASE DON'T!
I have to say this PROF is terrible!!!! The lecture notes is blank, and almost nothing had been written down in the notes after lecture! It is super hard to follow his lecture!!!!!!!

His lecture notes are useless for self-learning since there is little information in them but blanks. He explained each concepts clearly. But it's difficult to build a clear structure of all concepts on mind. But anyway, Mike himself is a nice guy.

This is the hardest course I've ever taken at SFU. This prof is really good for every class except CMPT 300! Had no problem failing a third of the class. Final and midterms were among the hardest I've ever seen and the assignments took weeks to complete. You're best off waiting to take this course with any other prof.
GPA booster. Simplest course ever taken. Got A without studying. Sample questions same as exam. Class start at least 30 minutes late than schedule. Grading is very easy.

His lectures are pretty good when he shows up on time and definitely interested in the subject material. Midterm was pretty easy but final was harder. Projects used from previous profs but you did learn something from doing them. Gave out lots of extensions which was annoying if you did the work in time. Pretty decent prof overall.

It was very easy course. Julian doesn't care much. He regularly came 30 minutes late and sometimes even 45 to 60 minutes late. Also cancelled few classes for personal reasons. He gives sample questions for midterm and final which are word to word same as his actual exam. Basically I will get easy A without learning anything.

Only criticism I have is some exam questions concepts were repeat from assignments. If you struggled on the assignments, it mirrored on the exams. Otherwise, Dr. Shea is a hero. He really knows the course material and how to teach it. Fast and efficient in office hours. Gave homework extensions based on class feedback. No regrets taking his class.

This prof is the reason people wouldn't wanna go to school. He lacks originality with copying all of his assignments from other universities which take up WAY too much time. The assignments should be worth 70% of the course instead of 50%. We should be focused on learning EFFICIENTLY, not having a lot of time consumed for the purpose of learning.

3 years at SFU and he's the best CMPT prof I've had. He knows what he is talking about AND he knows how to explain it clearly to students. He postpones assignments when requested so students can learn. Ryan Shea MAKES your job as a student easier by providing you with the necessary resources.
Hard course with tough prof. Assignments are Copied from graduate course ones. Exams ok.

Hazra's class was a rough ride. Assignments were very challenging, with a lot of tricky edge cases. The grading was harsh, and the midterm format change caught many off guard. Still, unstuck study was a lifesaver for prep. Overall, a tough but rewarding experience.
update she did not consider the 40% rule but still the assignment very heavy and there are lots of edge cases that need lots of time to discover and cover them
I won't say much cause every thing I was going to say is written by others here. This class was a pure waste of time.

I took CMPT 300 with her. Midterm and final exam is based on the assignments. Our TA was Hoda, she was an easy marker and gave answers to all the questions you got wrong . MAKE SURE TO DO THE OPTIONAL THEORY QUESTIONS!!! Those questions will be there for the midterm and final.

If you manage to understand her accent, the class will be very easy. Just read her slides, and the textbook if you do not understand sth. The exams are very long, but the questions are very easy and sometimes taken from the hw. I recommend.

Midterm questions are directly from homework, which is easy. You simply do the homework and you will get a good mark. Exam is pretty long, but the questions are not difficult. I didn't even buy the textbook and got a B. Her TA Hodaa was helpful and an easy marker.

She is one of the professors to avoid for CMPT 300. All of her notes are summarized points from the textbook. Her lecture slides has no pictures, just plain text. It is full of typos, mistakes and is not even complete. Her exams are pure memorization. The final exam consisted of rewriting blocks of text from the textbook. AVOID!!
Pros: material is pure memorization Cons: she is hard to understand, her notes suck, and the material is boring and long. So avoid skipping classes
Do not take this class with Prof Berenbrink unless you strongly dislike learning. Had difficulty responding to students questions.

I am a student from asia. It is horrible to understand her course with a strong european accent. Spelling mistakes happening in each slides. The programming assignments with no clue are assigned. But basically she is a nice person.

The lecture notes were of little help with the exams compared to the sample exams. A large number of students could not even submit a solution for the assignment because he refused to extend deadlines and he only provided cryptic, general support.

I'm a good student (4.0 GPA), but this guy was something else. Tests were heavy on critical-thinking, but the notes didn't prepare for that at all. Assignments had inconsistent difficulties (unlike what he promised). On many occasions, I was on the verge of a nervous breakdown because of his apathetic attitude to students. Take at your own risk.

Brian's a good guy and it's clear he wants his students to succeed. His assignments are brutal though. Lectures are focused just on theory so you are expected to learn a lot of C on your own. I imagine the course being much easier with Pearce but if you want to actually learn I strongly recommend taking it with Brian.

Brian is a great lecturer and EXTREMELY helpful in office hours, especially email. Kudos, very refreshing! That being said, his assignments were brutal and extremely time consuming. Be prepared to WORK A LOT. Exams were tough, you really need to know the material well. Blind memorization will get you nowhere.  Take it if you want a challenge.

Excessively strict grading policies led to 1/3 of the class failing. Unclear teaching style created unnecessary stress. Rules focus on removing students, not fostering a positive learning experience. His demotivating statements about failing students added to the dissatisfaction. The most dreadful professor I've ever come across. Be cautious.
Despite being a notoriously difficult course with hard assignments, this professor managed to make it all clear and understandable that attending every class was definitely worth every second.
Goated Prof

Amazing lectures, amazing slides. Hard assignments (4 of them, 2-3 in pairs). Fair, medium-hard exams. About same difficulty as 295 but it's okay because Harinder made this one of my favourite cmpt courses so far.

Best prof at SFU. Very professional and he sets the standard of what a professor should be like. Exams are fair and assignments are challenging but teach you a lot. Lecture ability is amazing and he is very accessible outside of class.

Love Kai! So sad he only taught one semester here. Assignments were not too bad and exams were straight forward as long as you went over the slides. Really wants you to understand OS's instead of giving head-banging problems to solve. He really cares if you learn the material and is very approachable. If you see a class with Kai, take it for sure!

He cares about his teaching. He puts effort into his slides and practices lectures. He is on a teaching exchange so his English skills are sometimes a barrier - mostly when it comes to answering student questions. When he is actually explaining material, it is ok to understand. The homework got exponentially harder after A1. Exams are from slides.

Great professor, his biggest issue is yapping, but he's a good professor, people who gave bad reviews should switch majors because the quizzes were reasonable and the assignments were moderate, it doesn't get easier from here on out.

He was very fair and a decent prof for CMPT 300. No final exam, only two quizzes, 2nd quiz was not cumulative and covered material past the first quiz only. Majority of grade came from the 4 assignments we had to do, which were pretty doable. I believe 454 is his passion class hence the higher difficulty, but would take again for a class like 300

Great prof. 4 assignments + 2 MT for the grading, no finals which is awesome. Very knowledgeable and slides are detailed and easy to comprehend. Only issue was that the class was way too early in the morning, but that's more of a scheduling issue. Would take a course with Tianzheng again!

Honestly one of the best lecturers I have encountered in my degree so far. Dr. Wang was engaging, open to questions and very approachable. He made a notoriously hard course a breeze. Even though CMPT 300 will no longer be offered I would recommended taking any course with him as a professor.
A good prof overall. He's not funny or exciting but he's clear, organized, and explains concepts well. Assignments might be a bit challenging but the grades he gives are fair.
